attribution: Transcript data: TalkMoves dataset (SumnerLab / Suresh et al.), CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, speaker-blinded before any model call.
caveats: The calculator checks a claim only when it can ground it; 'not checked' means outside its current coverage, not wrong.
Reading a speaker's move is interpretive. The model proposes the reading; only the claim verdicts are computed.
One automated read of one transcript — a starting point for your own reading, not a verdict on the discussion.

# tm_0077

U0001 S01: Um, do you remember the problem that, uh, I think everybody in  this class has now spent a bit of time working on the problem,  which is larger, three quarters or two thirds, and, if you decide  which one is larger you were asked, by how much.
U0002 S01: Do you  remember, how- Everybody here has worked on it, isn't that true?
U0003 S01: Raise your hand if you've worked on that problem?
U0004 S01: I believe  everybody, yeah.
U0005 S01: Some of you started a new one, but everybody  has worked on that.
U0006 S01: Ok, the question, how many of you have built  two models for that problem?
U0007 S01: For that problem, which is larger,  three quarters or two thirds, how many of you built two models?
U0008 S01: [Some students raise hands] Ok, how many of you have built more  than two models?
U0009 S01: So we have some,  you know, really differences here and some time to share, and  Gregory you built more than two also and Danielle, you've got two  models.
U0010 S01: Um, how many you think there are?
U0011 S01: How many models do  you think you can build?
U0012 S01: Michael.
U0013 S02: Um, if you know what you're doing and you know what strategy,  you could probably build, you could probably build one for every  single rod.
U0014 S01: What do you mean, one for every single rod, tell me what you  mean by that.
U0015 S02: You could, you could build a thing, you could build fractions of  every single rod if you know what you're doing and you have a  strategy or a secret that, that you know will work.
U0016 S01: What can be such a secret?
U0017 S02: That's what I was trying to figure out.
U0018 S01: That's what you were trying to figure out?
U0019 S01: Does anybody else have  any ideas about that?
U0020 S01: Those of you who built three models, do you  think that's all?
U0021 S01: Can you build more?
U0022 S01: This table here, Jackie and  James, Amy, you think there can be more?
U0023 S01: [mmm hmm] How  many do you think?
U0024 S03: Well, we got six
U0025 S01: You think that's it?
U0026 S04: [Shake heads] No.
U0027 S01: No?
U0028 S01: Anybody else?
U0029 S01: Any thoughts?
U0030 S01: [Meredith raises her hand]  Meredith?
U0031 S01: What do you think?
U0032 S05: Well, maybe if you, say you had a white rod, and you divided the  white rod, maybe you could make more models that way, if you  divided the white rods.
U0033 S01: So you're thinking if you had more, different size rods?
U0034 S05: Yeah, yeah.
U0035 S01: Like if you took the white rod and designed one smaller than the  white rod?
U0036 S01: [mmm hmm] Ok.
U0037 S01: Anybody else, what do you think?
U0038 S01: There was a theory, I know David's looking at what we built here,  David you had a theory that you were testing about uh building  some models.
U0039 S01: There were some models that everyone here built,  maybe we should put two of them on the overhead, if you can help  me do that, to compare which is bigger three quarters or two thirds.
U0040 S01: Um, to decide with the rods you have, what was the smallest model  you can build?
U0041 S01: With the rods you had available, not able to cut  them or make them different.
U0042 S01: Did you find- is there a smallest  model, when you compared three quarters or two thirds?
U0043 S01: Kelly, you think you have something, you want to come and help?
U0044 S01: Graham, you can come and help.
U0045 S06: I don't have fourths in my one that I made
U0046 S01: You don't have fourths?
U0047 S01: How about here?
U0048 S01: Amy?
U0049 S01: James?
U0050 S01: Jackie?
U0051 S01: [Amy James and Jackie come to OHP] Oh we're starting to get  think- we're starting thinking now.
U0052 S01: Ok, you want to tell the class about  what you have there?
U0053 S07: Ok, um, well um, we had this model and we uh this is two thirds  and three fourths and we think that three fourths is bigger by one  twelfth.
U0054 S07: [He separates two purple rods and three light green rods  from the original model to show the comparison].
U0055 S01: Questions, anybody?
U0056 S01: What's one twelfth?
U0057 S07: Because, um, twelve whites equal up to this.
U0058 S07: Let me show, here.
U0059 S07: [He puts two white rods on his model]
U0060 S01: Are you all convinced?
U0061 S04: But didn't you say to make a smaller one?
U0062 S01: Well I said you make the smallest one you can make?
U0063 S04: But I don't think that's the smallest one.
U0064 S01: Can you make a smaller one?
U0065 S01: It's an interesting question.
U0066 S01: If you  don't think there's a smaller one you should be able to show it, or at  least-.
U0067 S01: What do you think?
U0068 S07: [Figure O-15-38] Well, we just put twelve whites on there and it  takes one white to equal the two pinks, to the three, oh yeah, purple  to the three greens.
U0069 S07: So that's why we think it's one twelfth.

U0071 S01: How many of you agree with  that model?

U0073 S01: Raise your hand if you found the same model.
U0074 S01: [All  students visible raise hands] Ok, it looks as if everyone did.

U0080 S08: Smaller by size.

U0083 S09: Well, um, the orange and the red.
U0084 S01: Yeah that train we called one, right?
U0085 S01: And I guess the question is,  that train has a particular length, right?
U0086 S01: You can see the length of  that train?

U0089 S01: Right, Jessica?
U0090 S01: Jessica seems to think there's one that can  be made with a train that has length shorter than the one up there.
U0091 S01: So if you think you have an argument, Erik thinks he does, raise  your hand.
U0092 S01: I want you to think about this.
U0093 S01: You might want to talk  to your partner about your argument and see if your partner buys it.
U0094 S01: You don’t have to talk to your partner.
U0095 S01: Ok, we'll let you guys go  first and Erik is listening to see if he agrees.
U0096 S01: Ok.
U0097 S03: We say that there was no more, that you can't get a smaller one  because every one you use equals up to an orange and a red, and  the secret is that every one has three purples and four greens.
U0098 S03: And  so you can't possibly make one smaller because you won't be able  to fit, it won't work because every one you make equals up, equals  up to the orange and red.
U0099 S01: So you're telling me the six models that you made
U0100 S03: Were the same length.
U0101 S01: Were all the same length.
U0102 S01: In all of your cases, the, the, what you  called one had the same length as the orange and red.
U0103 S01: That's very  interesting.
U0104 S07: Well, well, we could make another model to show that.
U0105 S01: We believe you.
U0106 S01: Does anybody need to have that shown, what they  would've done?
U0107 S01: How many of you did that too?
U0108 S01: You found  different ways to show one, that had the same length as orange and  red,

U0110 S01: And what did, what was, how did she make her train?
U0111 S04: Yeah, that's what I was going to do.
U0112 S10: Blue, light green, and then the half is dark green, and the third is  purple and the fourths were the dark greens- were the light greens.
U0113 S04: See, that's what I did, I was gonna make one that was the same  exact size.
U0114 S01: Ok, so many of the models you made were really models where  your, what you called one, that train, really had the same length as  the orange and red.
U0115 S01: Is that all you can make?
U0116 S01: How do you know  that, that, that there's not one smaller?
U0117 S01: Erik?
U0118 S01: I'm still not convinced  that there's not one smaller.
U0119 S01: They didn't convince me.
U0120 S01: How would  you convince me?
U0121 S08: Well, see, I agree that, that, I agree with them just at the part that  there's no, there's no other smaller.
U0122 S08: I think, because at their model,  they use the twelfth as the white ones, and there's no rod smaller  than the white rod.
U0123 S08: So, therefore, if you make it a rod smaller than  it, they can't, you can't divide it into twelfths.
U0124 S01: Ok.
U0125 S01: Did you hear what he said?
U0126 S01: Yeah!
U0127 S08: Because the twelfths right here are the smallest rod possible.
U0128 S01: Ok, so
U0129 S08: Unless you made a new rod.
U0130 S01: So unless we use Meredith's idea of creating new rods that had,  that were smaller than the white rods, then you could make a  smaller model, Erik?
